page: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by a custom AWS Graviton processor. Built on the 64-bit ARM64 architecture, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-N1 core, this instance features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the m6g.medium delivers moderate single-core capabilities but sits in the lower percentiles for multi-core and memory-intensive operations. In stress-ng testing, the single-core performance achieved a score of 1499.79, placing it in the average tier, while its multi-core score remained identical due to the single-vCPU limitation. Memory bandwidth tests show 59.31 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 19.48 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression at 19.8 GB/sec, demonstrate weak relative performance. Redis SET operations reached 1.18 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput was recorded at 1.14 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6g.medium offers an accessible entry point into the ARM64 ecosystem on AWS, providing dedicated CPU resources without the credit-exhaustion risks of burstable instances. However, the single-core configuration and lack of local storage present clear tradeoffs for resource-heavy applications. This instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as low-traffic web servers, microservices, development environments, and basic utility scripting where high multi-threaded throughput is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of memory, a 32 MB L3 cache, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1499.79, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into lower percentiles due to the single-vCPU limitation. It achieves 1.18 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 1.14 GB/sec in static web serving. Qualitatively, the m6g.medium provides a cost-effective entry point for dedicated ARM64 compute resources. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, including microservices, small-scale web serving, and development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, suitable for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance with 1 Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 virtual machine offering balanced memory and compute resources for lightweight utility workloads and microservices.
bullet_points: Powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and a 32 MB L3 cache
Utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation
Delivers 1.18 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and development environments
